item.text: {"case_id":"mtj-015","score":1,"pass_criteria_met":false,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":false,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response wakes up to the right qualitative structure: it rejects a simplistic binary, identifies utilization and elasticity/volatility as the decisive axes, and projects a default hybrid path for the user's likely scenario. However it does not perform the required explicit extreme deduction at both ends, especially the 100% utilization versus 10% utilization contrast. It is more of a pragmatic comparison plus recommendation than a full EDSP-style extreme-axis derivation. It also avoids the fail signal of a generic comparison table."}
